title: Software
text:
A convenience Python package for using (any of) the \(\varphi\)-FEM scheme(s) (see e.g. this study or this chapter) in the FEniCSx high performance finite element computing plateform.



 











 







Authors: R. B., M. Duprez, K. Vuillemot
Developer: R. B.




Development: ongoing 💻
Maintenance: maintained ✅





Example of pieces of code using phiFEM:

github.com/JD26/FormOpt,
github.com/PhiFEM/publication_residual-estimates_fenicsx

title: Software
text:
fenicsx-error-estimation
An implementation of the Bank-Weiser a posteriori error estimator (introduced in this study) in the FEniCSx high performance finite element computing plateform. For a detailed description of its implementation see this study.
⚠️ This software is currently unmaintained and is not up-to-date with dolfinx latest version (I might update it in the future if I find the time).



 








 







Authors: R. B., J. S. Hale
Developers: R. B., J. S. Hale




Development: completed ✅
Maintenance: unmaintained ❌

title: Supervision
text:
Current

Louis Ducongé. 6 months internship at MIMESIS (Centre Inria de l’Université de Lorraine, Strasbourg, FR) in 2025 followed by a PhD since October 2025.
Topic: Shape optimization applied to surgical stents manufacturing.
Co-supervised with Dr. Stéphane Cotin, Dr. Michel Duprez and Pr. Yannick Privat.



Former

Sarah Ait-Said. 6 months internship at Université Laval (Québec, QC) in 2024.
Topic: Gradient reconstruction for multi-material problems.
Co-supervised with Pr. Jean Deteix.

title: Raphaël Bulle
text:
Since October 2024, I am a post-doc researcher in the team-project MIMESIS at Centre Inria de l’Université de Lorraine, in Strasbourg 🥨 France.
My current work is about a posteriori error estimation and adaptive mesh refinement for the immersed boundary method called \(\varphi\)-FEM. As a member of MIMESIS my work aims at providing numerical tools for biomecanics and biomedical simulations.







My research interests include:



 





FEM a posteriori error estimation







Immersed boundary FEM







Fractional PDEs




 




 





Shape optimization







Gradient reconstruction







Biomecanics
